patient_data.chief_complaint: Progressive left leg swelling, pain, and redness for 3 days
symptoms: Unilateral left leg swelling — 4cm circumference difference vs right
Dull aching pain in left calf and thigh, worse when standing
Warmth and erythema along the medial thigh
Pitting edema from ankle to mid-thigh
Positive Homans sign (calf pain on dorsiflexion)
patient_data.history: Long-haul flight (12 hours) 5 days ago, prostate cancer (in remission, completed treatment 6 months ago), sedentary lifestyle, hypertension

lab_results.compression_ultrasound: Non-compressible left common femoral and popliteal veins — acute thrombus confirmed

expected_output.primary_diagnosis: Acute Proximal Deep Vein Thrombosis - Left Lower Extremity
should_detect: CRITICAL: HIT history — DO NOT use any form of heparin (UFH or LMWH)
Anticoagulation: Direct oral anticoagulant (rivaroxaban 15mg BID x21d → 20mg daily)
Proximal DVT — high risk for PE, screen for PE symptoms
Cancer history: consider extended anticoagulation (3-6 months minimum)
Provoked by travel + cancer → unprovoked component possible → consider indefinite AC
Compression stockings for post-thrombotic syndrome prevention
IVC filter only if anticoagulation contraindicated
